title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Repository Trace: Change Review Ranges
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git change review ranges increment in a revision-query and change-discovery workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
Search commit messages, changed lines, content occurrences, authors, dates, and paths with pickaxe and regex evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Change Review Ranges while producing a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-change-review-ranges — Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
# Evidence: changed Git case 2
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
